perturbation.original_text: 4. PRODUCT WARRANTY. Contractor warrants to NICE that each of the Products manufactured, configured or tested by Contractor will have been manufactured, configured and tested in conformance with the Specifications therefor as provided by NICE and be free from defects in workmanship or material for the Warranty Period. It is clarified for the avoidance of doubt, that the aforesaid warranty of Contractor for the Products applies to the entire Product, including components and workmanship, except when the defect or malfunction results from the design, NICE Software or the Nice Components supplied by NICE under this Agreement if Contractor cannot obtain warranty service for same from the supplier (and Contractor shall notify NICE accordingly), for which NICE shall be responsible.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. PRODUCT WARRANTY. Contractor warrants to NICE that each of the Products manufactured, configured or tested by Contractor will have been manufactured, configured and tested in conformance with the Specifications therefor as provided by NICE. It is clarified for the avoidance of doubt, that the aforesaid warranty of Contractor for the Products applies only to the workmanship, and excludes components and defects/malfunctions resulting from the design, NICE Software or the Nice Components supplied by NICE under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This modification significantly reduces the warranty coverage. By limiting the warranty to only workmanship and explicitly excluding components and defects resulting from design or NICE-supplied components, it introduces a legal contradiction. Israeli law requires a product to be fit for purpose and free from defects. Omitting the components from the warranty reduces the level of guarantee in violation of consumer protection law.
perturbation.law_citation: Section 4 of the Israeli Consumer Protection Law
perturbation.law_explanation: Israeli Consumer Protection Law mandates products to be of reasonable quality and fit for their intended purpose. Section 4 of the law prohibits clauses that remove or substantially diminish a consumer's right to redress for defects, thus voiding clauses that remove essential guarantees about components, violating the consumer protection principles enshrined in Israeli law.
perturbation.justification: The modified text significantly reduces the warranty coverage by excluding components and defects from design or NICE-supplied components, which contradicts the principle that a product should be fit for its intended purpose. The Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation both state this violates consumer protection law, and removes guarantees about components.

perturbation.original_text: 6.5. INSPECTION. Products will be subject to inspection by NICE, or by certified NICE' distributors according to the following provisions, both prior to the Shipment Date while they are in the finished goods warehouse and after the Shipment Date. During the inspection, NICE may open cartons and boxes and unpack the contents for inspection. An inspection will be coordinated if possible. NICE may inform the Contractor if there is any mismatch in Product quantities or if any damage was caused to the shipment prior to delivery to NICE' freight forwarder in Contractor's facility. In case of a quantity mismatch between the quantity recorded on the packing documents and the actual quantity received, NICE will notify Contractor 22 of the mismatch, and, subject to verification by Contractor, Contractor will complete the missing quantity. In case where a shipment is found damaged prior to delivery to NICE' freight forwarder at Contractor's facility, the Contractor will replace or repair the Product and deliver it back to such NICE' freight forwarder at Contractor's facility, at Contractor's expense. If there was no mismatch in quantities or no damage to the shipment (as applicable), NICE will bear all risk and costs associated with the delivery of shipment to Contractor and back.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.5. INSPECTION. Products will be subject to inspection by NICE, or by certified NICE' distributors according to the following provisions, both prior to the Shipment Date while they are in the finished goods warehouse and after the Shipment Date. During the inspection, NICE may open cartons and boxes and unpack the contents for inspection. NICE may inform the Contractor if there is any mismatch in Product quantities or if any damage was caused to the shipment prior to delivery to NICE' freight forwarder in Contractor's facility. If there was no mismatch in quantities or no damage to the shipment (as applicable), NICE will bear all risk and costs associated with the delivery of shipment to Contractor and back.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the condition that Contractor needs to replace/repair a damaged product before delivery to NICE's freight forwarder removes an obligation from the contractor and thus provides less consumer protection.  This introduces a legal risk because it can violate warranty obligations and consumer protection laws, as the product is expected to be in merchantable condition when ownership transfers. The original text stipulated that if the product was damaged, the contractor would replace or repair. Removing this could be considered a violation of the warranty. This change makes the contract non-compliant by removing a safeguard for NICE.
perturbation.law_citation: Section 11 of the Israeli Sale Law
perturbation.law_explanation: The change removes the supplier's explicit obligation to repair or replace goods damaged before delivery to the freight forwarder. This conflicts with Section 11 of the Israeli Sale Law, which implicitly requires the seller to deliver goods that conform to the contract at the time of delivery, i.e., without damage. The original text protected the buyer by ensuring the goods are in good condition before shipment; the changed text removes that protection, thus potentially violating the buyer's rights under Israeli Sale Law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the contractor's obligation to repair or replace damaged products before delivery, potentially violating the buyer's rights under Israeli Sale Law. This aligns with the 'Omission Legal Contradiction' definition, where a contract removes legally mandated consumer protection, making it non-compliant. The Law Explanation notes that the original text protected the buyer by ensuring goods are in good condition before shipment, and the changed text removes that protection. Because the obligation to repair or replace was explicitly removed, this creates a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9.6. RMA. Should a Product fail to be in conformity with the above warranties during the Warranty Period, NICE shall deliver the Product to Contractor at its expense, Contractor shall repair or replace the Product at no charge (as set forth in Section 9.7 below), and will cover all shipment and delivery costs of therepaired or replaced Product from Contractor's facility to NICE' Customer location (whether in Israel or abroad). In the case that the determination according to Section 9.9 below, is that Contractor's warranty hereunder does not apply, NICE will bare all shipment and delivery costs of the Product/s and their return, and of any repair/replacement costs if requested by NICE, according to APPENDIX C. Detailed procedures to be executed by Contractor concerning the repair of defective/malfunctioning Products subject to the warranty hereunder are included in APPENDIX N - Return Material Authorization ("RMA").
perturbation.changed_text: 9.6. RMA. Should a Product fail to be in conformity with the above warranties during the Warranty Period, NICE shall deliver the Product to Contractor at its expense. In the case that the determination according to Section 9.9 below, is that Contractor's warranty hereunder does not apply, NICE will bare all shipment and delivery costs of the Product/s and their return, and of any repair/replacement costs if requested by NICE, according to APPENDIX C. Detailed procedures to be executed by Contractor concerning the repair of defective/malfunctioning Products subject to the warranty hereunder are included in APPENDIX N - Return Material Authorization ("RMA").
perturbation.explanation: This edit removes the explicit obligation for the Contractor to 'repair or replace the Product at no charge' and to 'cover all shipment and delivery costs of the repaired or replaced Product from Contractor's facility to NICE's Customer location'. This creates a **legal contradiction** with standard consumer protection laws (present in Israeli jurisdiction, as this contract specifies), that mandate the seller/manufacturer to repair or replace defective goods within a reasonable timeframe and without undue inconvenience to the consumer. By shifting the cost of repair/replacement (and shipping) onto NICE, this amended clause violates these established norms and exposes NICE to further legal risks.
perturbation.law_citation: Section 4(a)(5) of the Israeli Consumer Protection Law
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 4(a)(5) specifically prohibits stipulations that require consumers to bear unreasonable financial burdens when returning or replacing defective products. The omission of the contractor's responsibility to cover all costs associated with the repair or replacement of defective products directly contradicts this provision, as it imposes undue financial burden on NICE (the consumer in this case) and is, therefore, non-compliant with Israeli law.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the Contractor's obligation to repair or replace the defective product at no charge directly contradicts consumer protection laws. The Perturbation Explanation states the modification shifts the cost of repair/replacement onto NICE, violating established norms. The Law Explanation confirms that Section 4(a)(5) prohibits stipulations that require consumers to bear unreasonable financial burdens when returning or replacing defective products. Even though the scraped snippets are missing, the Law Explanation is sufficient to show that the omission creates a contradiction. This omission removes a core legal obligation, leading to clear non-compliance.
